title: Spring Onion Stuffed Tofu
description: A buttery and dense Venezuelan dish featuring spring onions stuffed into tofu cubes, cooked in a flavorful sauce for a one-pot meal that's perfect as a side or main course.

ingredients.name: 1 cup heavy cream or plant-based milk
1/4 cup finely chopped fresh spring onions

instructions: Cube the tofu into bite-sized pieces.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Sauté chopped onion until translucent and soft.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute, being careful not to burn it.
Add cumin and smoked paprika; stir well and cook for about 30 seconds.
Pour in the cream or milk, bring to a simmer, then add tofu cubes. Stir gently so as not to break them into smaller pieces.
Reduce heat to low-medium, cover, and let it braise until the sauce is thickened slightly, about 25-30 minutes.
Stir through chopped spring onions for last few minutes of cooking time. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course over rice or quinoa.
